title: Fix ShellCheck parse errors in prevent-credential-exposure.sh
description:
HIGH-11: SC1073/SC1078/SC1083 errors on the declare -A CREDENTIAL_PATTERNS associative array at hooks/prevent-credential-exposure.sh:71-96. The regex patterns inside single-quoted values confuse ShellCheck's parser, preventing analysis of the rest of the file (which includes the security-critical CLAUDE_SECURITY_OVERRIDE bypass).
Fix: Refactor credential patterns to avoid ShellCheck parse errors (e.g., use separate declare and assignment, or move patterns to a data file).
close_reason: Refactored CREDENTIAL_PATTERNS from compound array to individual assignments, fixed single-quote escaping with quote concatenation. bash -n passes on both copies.

title: Create CONTRIBUTING.md or remove all references
description:
HIGH-08: README.md links to ./docs/CONTRIBUTING.md at lines 490, 501, 538 in multiple places. No CONTRIBUTING.md exists anywhere in the repo.
Fix: Create docs/CONTRIBUTING.md with contribution guidelines, or remove all references from README.md.

title: Fix JSON injection in webhook notification payloads
description:
HIGH-02: All context-gathering and notification functions build JSON via heredocs with unescaped variable interpolation. Affected files: hooks/prevent-credential-exposure.sh:49-63, hooks/lib/error-handler.sh:273-289, hooks/pre-write-security.sh:36-46, hooks/subagent-trigger-simple.sh:57-76, hooks/on-error-debug.sh:37-65, hooks/lib/context-manager.sh:36-56.
Fix: Create json_escape() function in hooks/lib/file-utils.sh. Apply to all JSON payload construction. Add tests with adversarial inputs containing quotes and backslashes.
close_reason: Added json_escape() to file-utils.sh, applied across all 6 JSON-producing files in both root and claude-dev-toolkit copies. Also removed hostname leak from error-handler.sh and on-error-debug.sh.

title: Fix CI/CD security: pin actions, guard badge-update, fix hardcoded counts
description:
HIGH-03: All 4 workflows use tag-based action references (actions/checkout@v4, dorny/paths-filter@v2). Pin to full commit SHAs.
HIGH-04: test.yml:14 grants contents:write and badge-update step (lines 480-509) runs git push with no event guard. Add: if: github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'.
HIGH-09: npm-package-validation.yml:138 expects 13 active (actual: 15), line 143 expects 45 experimental (actual: 46 in source). Update counts or generate dynamically.
close_reason: Pinned all GitHub Actions to commit SHAs across 4 workflow files, added event guard to badge-update step, replaced hardcoded command counts with dynamic minimum checks

title: Synchronize xdevcontainer.md examples with setup-devcontainer.sh output
description: xdevcontainer.md contains hardcoded JSON/Dockerfile examples (lines 25-69) that differ from setup-devcontainer.sh output. Missing: customizations.vscode block, AWS env vars. Update to reference --dry-run or add CI validation.
close_reason: Synchronized xdevcontainer.md with setup-devcontainer.sh output: added customizations.vscode block, AWS env vars, updated Dockerfile with security labels and healthcheck, added new flags (--strict, --allow-domain, env var support).

title: Create xcontinue slash command for execution plan continuation
description:
Create a slash command for execution plan continuation, addressing the 10+ sessions of 'follow execution plan' identified in the usage report as a top activity with high friction from lost context.

**PREREQUISITE: Read task claude-code-8g8 first for dual-write rules.**

**Implementation:**
- File: slash-commands/active/xcontinue.md
- ALSO create: claude-dev-toolkit/commands/active/xcontinue.md (identical copy)

**Command file must follow the existing frontmatter format. Reference existing command for structure:**
See slash-commands/active/xtest.md or slash-commands/active/xgit.md for the exact format:
```markdown
---
description: "Brief command description"
tags: ["category1", "category2"]
---

# Command Title

## Description
...

## Usage Examples
...

## Implementation
[The actual instructions Claude follows when the command is invoked]
```

**Command behavior when invoked as /xcontinue:**
1. Search for execution plan files: glob for *plan*, *PLAN*, EXECUTION-PLAN*, *execution*plan*
2. If no plan found: tell user no plan file was found, suggest creating one with a template
3. If plan found: read it, parse completed (checked items [x]) vs remaining (unchecked [ ]) tasks
4. Display progress summary: X of Y tasks complete, next task description
5. Execute the next incomplete task
6. After completing: run validation (tests, lint if configured)
7. Update plan file: mark task as [x] complete with timestamp
8. Tell user: 'Task N complete. Run /clear then /xcontinue to proceed to the next task.'
9. If all tasks complete: congratulate and summarize what was accomplished

**Files to create/modify:**
1. Create slash-commands/active/xcontinue.md
2. Copy to claude-dev-toolkit/commands/active/xcontinue.md
3. Update claude-dev-toolkit/tests/test_npm_package_completeness.js:
   - Line 96-98: Change active command count from 13 to 14 (or current count + 1)
   - Line 230-234: Change DUPLICATE active command count to match
   NOTE: If other slash command tasks (6ck, 7wz) run first, the count will already be higher. Read the current value in the file before updating.

**Verification:**
```bash
# File exists in both locations
test -f slash-commands/active/xcontinue.md && echo 'OK: repo' || echo 'MISSING: repo'
test -f claude-dev-toolkit/commands/active/xcontinue.md && echo 'OK: npm' || echo 'MISSING: npm'
# Frontmatter is valid
head -3 slash-commands/active/xcontinue.md | grep -q '^---' && echo 'OK: frontmatter' || echo 'BAD: frontmatter'
# npm tests pass
cd claude-dev-toolkit && npm test
```
close_reason: Created xcontinue.md with plan discovery, progress parsing, task execution, and handoff protocol. Dual-written, test count updated to 14.

title: Extract method: print_next_steps() in setup-devcontainer.sh
description: print_next_steps() is 44 lines with mixed responsibilities. Extract into: print_configuration_summary(), print_usage_instructions(), print_security_notes(). Each function under 15 lines.
close_reason: Refactored into smaller focused functions: generate_firewall_script(), generate_base_dockerfile(), generate_dockerfile_footer(), print_configuration_summary(), print_usage_instructions(), print_allowed_domains(), print_security_notes(). All tests passing.

title: Remove or implement 13 stub hooks, clean security templates
description:
HIGH-01: 13 of 22 hooks are identical 62-line stubs with TODO comments: audit-bash-commands.sh, backup-before-edit.sh, cleanup-on-stop.sh, handle-notifications.sh, log-all-operations.sh, pre-compact-backup.sh, prompt-analysis.sh, prompt-security-scan.sh, security-session-init.sh, session-cleanup.sh, session-init.sh, subagent-cleanup.sh, validate-changes.sh.
MED-01: Security-named stubs (prompt-security-scan.sh, security-session-init.sh, audit-bash-commands.sh) are configured in templates/security-focused-settings.json creating a false audit trail.
Fix: Either implement meaningful logic or remove from templates. Never ship security-named stubs in a security-focused template.

title: Fix devcontainer firewall: add lifecycle hook and NET_ADMIN capability
description:
CRIT-01: setup-devcontainer.sh generates firewall script but nothing invokes it at container start. No postStartCommand in devcontainer.json, and --cap-drop=ALL prevents iptables.
Fix: Add postStartCommand to run setup-firewall.sh. Add --cap-add=NET_ADMIN to runArgs. Add test that validates lifecycle hook exists.
MED-20: Document ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var requirement in devcontainer setup instructions.
MED-21: Replace domain-name iptables rules (setup-devcontainer.sh:312) with IP ranges — domains resolve once at rule creation time, becoming stale when CDN IPs rotate.
close_reason: Added postStartCommand for firewall script in generated devcontainer.json, added --cap-add=NET_ADMIN to runArgs, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY already documented in check_prerequisites

title: Create universal pre-commit test runner hook
description:
The usage report shows 192 'command failed' errors and 17 'buggy code' friction events. Create a universal test runner hook that auto-detects the project's test framework and gates commits.

**PREREQUISITE: Read task claude-code-8g8 first. Complete task claude-code-d6g first (it updates test counts you'll also modify).**

**Implementation:**
- File: hooks/pre-commit-test-runner.sh
- ALSO create: claude-dev-toolkit/hooks/pre-commit-test-runner.sh (identical copy)

**Hook must follow this exact metadata format (required by hook-metadata-service.js):**
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# Claude Code Hook: Pre-Commit Test Runner
#
# Description: Auto-detects test framework and runs tests before commits
# Purpose: Prevent commits with failing tests by validating the full test suite
# Trigger: PreToolUse
# Blocking: Yes
# Tools: Bash
# Author: Claude Dev Toolkit
# Version: 1.0.0
# Category: security
#
# This hook validates code quality by running the project's test suite
# before allowing commit operations to proceed.
```

**CRITICAL: Security keyword requirement**
hook-validator.js requires at least one of: credential, security, validate, check, prevent. Use 'validate' and 'check' naturally in comments and log messages.

**CRITICAL: Input mechanism**
Hooks receive context via environment variables:
- \$CLAUDE_TOOL — the tool name (check if it's a git commit operation)
- \$CLAUDE_FILE — not relevant for this hook
- \$CLAUDE_CONTENT — not relevant for this hook

**Auto-detection logic (detection order):**
1. pytest: check for pytest.ini, setup.cfg with [tool:pytest], or pyproject.toml with [tool.pytest]
2. jest/vitest: check for package.json with 'test' script containing jest/vitest
3. go test: check for go.mod
4. npm test: check for package.json with 'test' script (generic fallback)
5. cargo test: check for Cargo.toml
6. mix test: check for mix.exs

**Behavior:**
- If CLAUDE_TOOL is not a commit-related Bash command, exit 0 immediately
- If no test framework detected: log warning, exit 0 (allow commit)
- If tests pass: log success, exit 0
- If tests fail: output summary (pass/fail counts, first failure), exit 1 (block)

**Files to create/modify:**
1. Create hooks/pre-commit-test-runner.sh
2. Copy to claude-dev-toolkit/hooks/pre-commit-test-runner.sh
3. chmod +x both files
4. Update hooks/README.md — add entry for pre-commit-test-runner
5. Update claude-dev-toolkit/tests/test_npm_package_completeness.js:
   - Line 115-122: Add 'hooks/pre-commit-test-runner.sh' to expectedHooks array
   - Line 267-276: Add same to DUPLICATE expectedHooks array
   (NOTE: If d6g already updated these arrays, just append to the updated arrays)
6. Add hook reference to templates/comprehensive-settings.json under PreToolUse with matcher 'Bash'
7. Copy updated comprehensive-settings.json to claude-dev-toolkit/templates/
8. Add hook reference to templates/security-focused-settings.json under PreToolUse with matcher 'Bash'
9. Copy updated security-focused-settings.json to claude-dev-toolkit/templates/

**Verification:**
```bash
# Hook exists and is executable in both locations
test -x hooks/pre-commit-test-runner.sh && echo 'OK: repo' || echo 'MISSING: repo'
test -x claude-dev-toolkit/hooks/pre-commit-test-runner.sh && echo 'OK: npm' || echo 'MISSING: npm'
# Templates are valid JSON
python3 -c 'import json; json.load(open("templates/comprehensive-settings.json"))'
python3 -c 'import json; json.load(open("templates/security-focused-settings.json"))'
# npm tests pass
cd claude-dev-toolkit && npm test
```
close_reason: Created pre-commit-test-runner.sh with 6-framework auto-detection. Dual-written, added to settings templates, tests updated.

title: Fix critical documentation drift: CLAUDE.md, README.md, /xhelp
description:
CRIT-03: Remove 20+ references to 5 nonexistent root scripts (setup.sh, deploy.sh, verify-setup.sh, validate-commands.sh, configure-claude-code.sh). Update to npm CLI equivalents.
CRIT-04: Rewrite CLAUDE.md lib/ listing — claims 7 modules (auth.sh, config.sh, etc.) that don't exist. Only lib/logging.sh exists (orphaned).
CRIT-05: Create slash-commands/active/xhelp.md OR update all references (README:135,385, CLAUDE.md:221, postinstall.js:157, verify-command.js:372, setup-command.js:68).
HIGH-14: Add missing dirs to CLAUDE.md structure: claude-dev-toolkit/, subagents/, scripts/, setup-devcontainer.sh, .github/, tests/.
HIGH-07: Fix 4 different command counts: replace stale 13/44/59 with correct 15/46/61 across README.md, docs/npm-package-guide.md.
MED-14: Reconcile '30 seconds' (README:101) vs '2 minutes' (README:563) time claims.
MED-22: Update docs/npm-package-guide.md counts from 57/13/44 to 61/15/46.

title: Create xexplore slash command for exploration-first workflows
description:
Create a slash command for exploration-first workflows. The usage report shows 244 Agent tool calls and identifies that friction spikes when Claude acts without exploring first.

**PREREQUISITE: Read task claude-code-8g8 first for dual-write rules.**

**Implementation:**
- File: slash-commands/active/xexplore.md
- ALSO create: claude-dev-toolkit/commands/active/xexplore.md (identical copy)

**Command file must follow the existing frontmatter format:**
See slash-commands/active/xtest.md for structure reference:
```markdown
---
description: "Brief command description"
tags: ["category1", "category2"]
---

# Command Title

## Description
...

## Usage Examples
...

## Implementation
[The actual instructions Claude follows]
```

**Command behavior when invoked as /xexplore [topic]:**
1. Take a topic/keyword/area as input argument
2. Search the codebase comprehensively:
   - File names matching the topic (glob)
   - File contents referencing the topic (grep)
   - Config files (*.json, *.yaml, *.yml, *.toml, *.env*) mentioning the topic
   - Test files related to the topic
   - Documentation mentioning the topic
3. Report structured inventory grouped by category:
   - Source files (with line numbers for key matches)
   - Tests
   - Configuration
   - Documentation
4. Does NOT make any changes — read-only exploration
5. Handles 'nothing found' gracefully with suggestions to broaden the search

**Files to create/modify:**
1. Create slash-commands/active/xexplore.md
2. Copy to claude-dev-toolkit/commands/active/xexplore.md
3. Update claude-dev-toolkit/tests/test_npm_package_completeness.js:
   - Line 96-98: Increment active command count by 1 (read current value first)
   - Line 230-234: Increment DUPLICATE active command count to match
   NOTE: If other slash command tasks (3og, 7wz) have already run, count may already be incremented. Always read the current value before updating.

**Verification:**
```bash
test -f slash-commands/active/xexplore.md && echo 'OK: repo' || echo 'MISSING: repo'
test -f claude-dev-toolkit/commands/active/xexplore.md && echo 'OK: npm' || echo 'MISSING: npm'
head -3 slash-commands/active/xexplore.md | grep -q '^---' && echo 'OK: frontmatter' || echo 'BAD: frontmatter'
cd claude-dev-toolkit && npm test
```

title: Create xverify slash command for pre-action verification
description:
Create a slash command for pre-action verification. Addresses the #1 friction source: 'wrong approach' (29x), often from Claude fabricating URLs, account IDs, or asset paths.

**PREREQUISITE: Read task claude-code-8g8 first for dual-write rules.**

**Implementation:**
- File: slash-commands/active/xverify.md
- ALSO create: claude-dev-toolkit/commands/active/xverify.md (identical copy)

**Command file must follow the existing frontmatter format:**
See slash-commands/active/xtest.md for structure reference:
```markdown
---
description: "Brief command description"
tags: ["category1", "category2"]
---

# Command Title

## Description
...

## Usage Examples
...

## Implementation
[The actual instructions Claude follows]
```

**Command behavior when invoked as /xverify [file-or-directory]:**
1. Take a target file or directory as input (default: current directory)
2. Scan target files for external references:
   - URLs (http/https patterns)
   - Relative file path references (./path, ../path)
   - Account/property IDs (GA-*, UA-*, AKIA*, etc.)
   - API endpoints
   - Asset paths (images, logos, icons referenced in code)
3. For each reference found, attempt verification:
   - File paths: glob/read to check they exist on disk
   - URLs: check if they appear in config files, READMEs, or .env files in the repo
   - IDs: flag anything that looks like a placeholder (your-*, xxx, TODO, REPLACE_ME)
4. Output structured report:
   - VERIFIED: reference exists in repo or on disk
   - UNVERIFIED: reference not found (may be external — not necessarily wrong)
   - SUSPICIOUS: looks like a placeholder or fabricated value
5. Non-blocking — reports findings, user decides whether to proceed

**Exclusions (no false positives):**
- Skip localhost, 127.0.0.1, example.com in test/example files
- Skip node_modules, .git, vendor directories
- Skip binary files

**Files to create/modify:**
1. Create slash-commands/active/xverify.md
2. Copy to claude-dev-toolkit/commands/active/xverify.md
3. Update claude-dev-toolkit/tests/test_npm_package_completeness.js:
   - Line 96-98: Increment active command count by 1 (read current value first)
   - Line 230-234: Increment DUPLICATE active command count to match
   NOTE: If other slash command tasks (3og, 6ck) have already run, count may already be incremented. Always read the current value before updating.

**Verification:**
```bash
test -f slash-commands/active/xverify.md && echo 'OK: repo' || echo 'MISSING: repo'
test -f claude-dev-toolkit/commands/active/xverify.md && echo 'OK: npm' || echo 'MISSING: npm'
head -3 slash-commands/active/xverify.md | grep -q '^---' && echo 'OK: frontmatter' || echo 'BAD: frontmatter'
cd claude-dev-toolkit && npm test
```

title: Ensure all new artifacts are mirrored in claude-dev-toolkit npm package
description:
CRITICAL: This repo has a dual-directory structure. Source files live in slash-commands/, hooks/, and templates/ at the repo root, but the npm package (claude-dev-toolkit/) maintains its own copies under commands/, hooks/, and templates/. The npm postinstall.js copies from the package's directories to ~/.claude/.

**This task is a READ-FIRST prerequisite for all other tasks.** It defines the rules every other task must follow. Do NOT implement code — just read and understand this before starting any other task.

**Current sync status:**
- commands/active/ and slash-commands/active/ are IN SYNC (13 commands each, identical)
- commands/experiments/ and slash-commands/experiments/ are IN SYNC (45 each)
- hooks/ are IN SYNC (same 8 hook scripts + lib/ subdirectory)
- templates/ differ: npm package is MISSING global-claude.md and the *-OLD.json files (expected — OLD files shouldn't ship)

**RULE 1: Dual-write every new file**
Any new file created in slash-commands/active/, slash-commands/experiments/, hooks/, or templates/ MUST also be copied to the corresponding directory under claude-dev-toolkit/:
- slash-commands/active/x*.md → claude-dev-toolkit/commands/active/x*.md
- hooks/*.sh → claude-dev-toolkit/hooks/*.sh
- templates/* → claude-dev-toolkit/templates/*

**RULE 2: Update hardcoded test counts in test_npm_package_completeness.js**
File: claude-dev-toolkit/tests/test_npm_package_completeness.js
This file has HARDCODED counts that MUST be updated when adding files:
- Line 96-98: 'npm pack includes exactly 13 active command files' → update count
- Line 101-103: 'npm pack includes exactly 45 experimental command files' → update count if changed
- Line 115-122: expectedHooks array — add new hook filenames
- Line 125-127: subagent count (26) — update if changed
- Line 230-234: DUPLICATE active/experimental count check (filesystem-based) → update same counts
- Line 244-248: DUPLICATE subagent count check → update if changed
- Line 267-276: DUPLICATE expectedHooks array → add same new hook filenames
- Line 106-112: expectedTemplates array — add if new templates go in npm package

**RULE 3: New hooks must follow exact metadata format**
The hook-metadata-service.js parses metadata from the first 20 lines of each hook script. Required format:
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# Claude Code Hook: [Human-Readable Name]
#
# Description: [What this hook does]
# Purpose: [Detailed purpose]
# Trigger: PreToolUse
# Blocking: Yes
# Tools: Edit, Write, MultiEdit
# Author: Claude Dev Toolkit
# Version: 1.0.0
# Category: security
```

**RULE 4: Hook validator requires security keywords**
hook-validator.js (lines 203-213) requires the hook file to contain at least one of these keywords (case-insensitive): credential, security, validate, check, prevent. Hooks that don't naturally contain these will fail validation.

**RULE 5: Hooks receive input via environment variables, NOT stdin**
Claude Code sets these env vars before invoking hooks:
- \$CLAUDE_TOOL — tool name (Edit, Write, Bash, etc.)
- \$CLAUDE_FILE — file path being operated on
- \$CLAUDE_CONTENT — content being written (fallback when file doesn't exist yet)
- \$CLAUDE_VERSION, \$CLAUDE_PROJECT, \$CLAUDE_SESSION_ID — additional context

**RULE 6: Hooks must be executable**
chmod +x on all .sh files. The postinstall.js does this automatically (line 191: fs.chmodSync(targetPath, '755')).

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- This task is complete when it has been read. It produces no code.
- Mark as done immediately after reading — it serves as documentation for all subsequent tasks.
close_reason: Read-only prerequisite acknowledged. All 6 rules internalized: dual-write, test counts, hook metadata format, security keywords, env vars input, chmod.

The claude-dev-toolkit/ copy retains stricter checks. description:
LOW-01: Delete empty test-suite.json at repo root (0 bytes, orphaned).
LOW-02: Remove ubuntu-test-results.zip from git tracking. Add *.zip to .gitignore.
LOW-03: Remove 70+ committed test result files in tests/test-results/. Add to .gitignore.

LOW-05: Remove docs/.DS_Store. Add .DS_Store to .gitignore.
LOW-07: Add __pycache__/ to .gitignore.

LOW-08: Fix log function  → "$@" in stub hooks.

title: Eliminate duplicate file maintenance between repo root and npm package
description:
HIGH-10: Every hook, lib module, and subagent is duplicated identically between repo root and claude-dev-toolkit/. No automated sync exists. The copies have already diverged (see CRIT-06 where security validation was commented out in repo root but not npm copy).
Files: hooks/ vs claude-dev-toolkit/hooks/, subagents/ vs claude-dev-toolkit/subagents/.
Fix: Establish a single source of truth. Use a build step (symlinks, copy script, or npm files with repo-root references) to eliminate duplication.

title: Fix shell minor issues: $* quoting, hostname leak, unwired tests
description:
LOW-08: Multiple stub hooks use log() { echo ... $* >> LOG_FILE } — arguments with spaces could split. Change to "$@" for correct argument handling.
LOW-09: hooks/lib/error-handler.sh:284 includes $(hostname) in webhook notification payloads, leaking infrastructure details to external endpoints. Remove or redact hostname from external payloads.
LOW-10: tests/test_logging.sh and tests/test_setup_devcontainer.sh are well-written unit tests but not wired into CI. run-all-tests.sh only discovers .js files. Update test runner to also discover and run .sh test files.

title: Implement uninstall mechanism for claude-dev-toolkit
description:
CRIT-07: npm uninstall only removes the package. Left behind: ~/.claude/commands/, ~/.claude/hooks/, ~/.claude/subagents/, settings.json changes, ~/.claude/backups/. README:396 claims 'removes everything' — false.
Fix: Implement 'claude-commands uninstall' command and preuninstall npm hook per specs/npm-distribution-requirements.md:153 and docs/npm-distribution-plan.md:47,64. Update README to link to docs/manual-uninstall-install-guide.md.

title: Fix test claims, subagent validation, CLI flags, and Windows support claim
description:
MED-10: CLAUDE.md global instructions say 100% tests must pass but tests primarily validate file existence and markdown patterns (70% smoke tests). 100% pass rate is trivially achievable. Acknowledge the test depth gap and add functional tests.
MED-11: 26 subagent definitions in subagents/*.md have zero validation or testing. No tests validate frontmatter structure, required fields, or consistency.
MED-12: claude-dev-toolkit/bin/claude-commands:22-47 defines --active and --experiments flags for list command but the action handler never reads options.active or options.experiments. Always lists all commands. Implement the filtering logic or remove the flags.
MED-13: docs/npm-package-guide.md:130 claims Works on macOS, Linux, and Windows but postinstall.js:167 uses __dirname.replace('/scripts', '') — Unix-specific path separator. Either implement cross-platform path handling (path.dirname()) or add macOS/Linux only disclaimer.

title: Add functional tests for hooks, hooks/lib, and slash commands
description:
HIGH-05: 22 hooks have zero dedicated functional tests. 8 hooks/lib modules have zero unit tests (CI only does bash -n). 15 active slash commands have zero behavioral tests.
HIGH-06: ~80+ REQ-xxx requirements, only 8 have tests (~10% coverage). Create traceability matrix.
MED-10: '100% tests must pass' claim misleading when 70% are smoke tests.
LOW-10: test_logging.sh and test_setup_devcontainer.sh exist but aren't wired into CI — run-all-tests.sh only discovers .js files.
Priority: Start with security-critical hooks (prevent-credential-exposure.sh, pre-write-security.sh), then hooks/lib modules, then commands.
close_reason: Duplicate of claude-code-x51 (HIGH-05, HIGH-06)

title: Eliminate repo/npm file duplication, remove dead code
description:
HIGH-10: Every hook, lib module, and subagent is duplicated between repo root (hooks/, subagents/) and claude-dev-toolkit/ (hooks/, subagents/). No automated sync — copies have already diverged (CRIT-06). Establish single source of truth via build step, symlinks, or npm files array.
HIGH-13: Delete dead code — lib/package-manager-service.js (269 lines, zero importers, superseded by lib/services/package-manager-service.js) and installation-instruction-generator-backup.js (578 lines, pre-refactor snapshot).
close_reason: Duplicate of claude-code-anl (HIGH-10)

title: Fix broken hook references in settings templates
description:
The comprehensive-settings.json and security-focused-settings.json templates reference 13 unique hook scripts that don't exist in hooks/. Anyone copying these templates gets errors.

**PREREQUISITE: Read task claude-code-8g8 first for dual-write rules, hook metadata format, and test update requirements.**

**Missing hooks by template:**

comprehensive-settings.json (11 missing):
1. backup-before-edit.sh (PreToolUse, Edit|Write|MultiEdit)
2. audit-bash-commands.sh (PreToolUse, Bash)
3. log-all-operations.sh (PostToolUse, *)
4. validate-changes.sh (PostToolUse, Edit|Write|MultiEdit)
5. handle-notifications.sh (Notification, *)
6. prompt-analysis.sh (UserPromptSubmit, *)
7. cleanup-on-stop.sh (Stop, *)
8. subagent-cleanup.sh (SubagentStop, *)
9. session-cleanup.sh (SessionEnd, *)
10. pre-compact-backup.sh (PreCompact, *)
11. session-init.sh (SessionStart, *)

security-focused-settings.json (2 additional unique):
12. prompt-security-scan.sh (UserPromptSubmit, *)
13. security-session-init.sh (SessionStart, *)

**Implementation requirements for each skeleton hook:**

Each skeleton MUST follow this exact template (from hook-metadata-service.js requirements):
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# Claude Code Hook: [Human-Readable Name]
#
# Description: [One-line description]
# Purpose: [What security/validation benefit this provides]
# Trigger: [PreToolUse|PostToolUse|Notification|UserPromptSubmit|Stop|SubagentStop|SessionEnd|PreCompact|SessionStart]
# Blocking: [Yes|No]
# Tools: [Edit, Write, MultiEdit|Bash|*]
# Author: Claude Dev Toolkit
# Version: 1.0.0
# Category: security

##################################
# Configuration
##################################
HOOK_NAME="[hook-name]"
LOG_FILE="\$HOME/.claude/logs/[hook-name].log"

mkdir -p "\$(dirname "\$LOG_FILE")"
chmod 700 "\$(dirname "\$LOG_FILE")"
touch "\$LOG_FILE"
chmod 600 "\$LOG_FILE"

##################################
# Logging
##################################
log() {
    echo "[\$(date +'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')] [\$HOOK_NAME] \$*" >> "\$LOG_FILE"
}

##################################
# Main
##################################
main() {
    local tool_name="\${CLAUDE_TOOL:-unknown}"
    local file_path="\${CLAUDE_FILE:-}"
    log "Hook triggered for tool: \$tool_name, file: \$file_path"
    # [Skeleton: add validation/check logic here]
    log "Security check passed"
    exit 0
}

trap 'log "Hook failed on line \$LINENO"' ERR
main "\$@"
```

**CRITICAL: Security keyword requirement**
hook-validator.js requires each hook to contain at least one of: credential, security, validate, check, prevent (case-insensitive). Every skeleton must include at least one of these words in its comments or logic. Use 'security check' or 'validate' naturally in the log messages.

**CRITICAL: Input mechanism**
Hooks receive input via environment variables (\$CLAUDE_TOOL, \$CLAUDE_FILE, \$CLAUDE_CONTENT), NOT stdin.

**Files to create/modify:**
1. Create 13 new hook scripts in hooks/ (listed above)
2. Copy each to claude-dev-toolkit/hooks/
3. chmod +x all new scripts
4. Update hooks/README.md with descriptions of all 13 new hooks
5. Update claude-dev-toolkit/tests/test_npm_package_completeness.js:
   - Line 115-122: Add all 13 new hook filenames to expectedHooks array
   - Line 267-276: Add same filenames to DUPLICATE expectedHooks array
6. Verify templates remain valid JSON (no changes to template content needed — the hook scripts now exist)

**Verification commands:**
```bash
# All hooks exist in both locations
for f in backup-before-edit audit-bash-commands log-all-operations validate-changes handle-notifications prompt-analysis cleanup-on-stop subagent-cleanup session-cleanup pre-compact-backup session-init prompt-security-scan security-session-init; do
  test -x hooks/\$f.sh && test -x claude-dev-toolkit/hooks/\$f.sh && echo "OK: \$f" || echo "MISSING: \$f"
done
# Tests pass
cd claude-dev-toolkit && npm test
```
close_reason: Created 13 skeleton hooks, dual-written to claude-dev-toolkit/, updated test expectedHooks arrays, refactored test file for complexity, updated hooks/README.md

MED-09: Jest declared as dependency in tests/package.json:22 and claude-dev-toolkit/package.json:63 but never used. All tests use custom runners with hand-rolled assert logic. Zero use of Jest APIs. Either migrate to Jest or remove the dependency.

Standardize all 26 definitions. Move debug-context.md to docs/.

title: Add verification and zero-error rules to global CLAUDE.md template
description:
Add three new sections to templates/global-claude.md addressing the top friction sources from the usage report: wrong approach (29x), misunderstood request (22x), buggy code (17x).

**PREREQUISITE: Read task claude-code-8g8 first for dual-write rules.**

**Sections to add (insert after the existing PHASE 2 section, before PHASE 3):**

1. **VERIFICATION BEFORE ACTION** — New section 2.7:
   - Before referencing any URL, file path, account ID, asset, or external identifier: search the repo for the actual value
   - Never fabricate or guess URLs, IDs, or resource paths — verify by reading config files or running lookup commands
   - If the resource can't be found in the repo, ask the user
   - For external services (analytics, cloud providers, etc.), read config files — never assume account/property IDs

2. **ZERO-ERROR POLICY** — New section 2.8:
   - After any code change, run the full test suite — not a subset
   - ALL tests must pass with zero errors, including pre-existing ones
   - Do not mark a task complete until the test suite is fully green
   - If pre-existing tests are failing, fix them or explicitly flag them to the user — never silently skip

3. **PLATFORM-SPECIFIC FORMATTING** — New section 2.9:
   - When generating content for external platforms, respect formatting constraints:
     - LinkedIn: Plain text only — no markdown, no code blocks, no numbered lists with markdown syntax
     - Slack: Use Slack mrkdwn — bold = *text*, italic = _text_, code = \`text\`
     - GitHub: Standard GitHub-flavored markdown
     - HTML emails: Inline styles only, no external CSS references
   - If the target platform is not specified, ask before generating

**Rules for these sections:**
- No PII, no user-specific paths, no tool-specific references (no Beads, no specific IDE)
- Keep concise — checklist format matching existing Phase 2 style
- Do not modify any existing sections

**Files to create/modify:**
1. Edit templates/global-claude.md — add sections 2.7, 2.8, 2.9
2. Copy updated file to claude-dev-toolkit/templates/global-claude.md
   NOTE: This file currently does NOT exist in claude-dev-toolkit/templates/. Create it.
3. Update claude-dev-toolkit/tests/test_npm_package_completeness.js:
   - Line 106-112: Add 'templates/global-claude.md' to expectedTemplates array
   - Line 251-258: Add 'global-claude.md' to DUPLICATE expectedTemplates check
4. Update claude-dev-toolkit/templates/README.md if it lists available templates

**Verification:**
```bash
# File exists in both locations
test -f templates/global-claude.md && echo 'OK: repo' || echo 'MISSING: repo'
test -f claude-dev-toolkit/templates/global-claude.md && echo 'OK: npm' || echo 'MISSING: npm'
# Existing sections unchanged (spot check)
grep -c 'PHASE 1' templates/global-claude.md  # should be 1
grep -c 'PHASE 2' templates/global-claude.md  # should be 1
# New sections present
grep -c 'VERIFICATION BEFORE ACTION' templates/global-claude.md  # should be 1
grep -c 'ZERO-ERROR POLICY' templates/global-claude.md  # should be 1
grep -c 'PLATFORM-SPECIFIC FORMATTING' templates/global-claude.md  # should be 1
# npm tests pass
cd claude-dev-toolkit && npm test
```
close_reason: Added sections 2.7, 2.8, 2.9 to global-claude.md. Dual-written to npm package. Test expectedTemplates updated.

JS: oidc-command.js (739), test_oidc_command.js (671), installation-instruction-generator-backup.js (578), error-recovery-system.js (562), system-requirements-checker.js (557).

Zero import references to old file. installation-instruction-generator-backup.js (578 lines) is a pre-refactor snapshot committed as production code.
Fix: Delete dead code files immediately. Decompose files over 500 lines using Extract Class/Extract Module patterns.

MED-03: Fix path traversal gaps in file-utils.sh:342-374 — validate_path_safety() doesn't resolve symlinks and allows all relative paths. Use realpath.

MED-17: Remove unused ShellCheck-flagged variables across config-constants.sh (6 vars), error-handler.sh:190,220, execution-engine.sh:537, context-manager.sh:99, subagent-validator.sh:107.

description:
MED-11: 26 subagent definitions in subagents/ have zero validation or testing. No tests check frontmatter structure, required fields, or consistency. Combined with CRIT-06 (content security commented out), malicious .md files could influence Claude behavior.
Fix: Add validation tests for subagent definitions (frontmatter, name/description/tools fields). Re-enable content security checks in subagent-validator.sh. Add warning when project-level subagents override user-level ones.

title: Create verify-before-edit hook for reference validation
description:
Create a PreToolUse hook that warns about potentially fabricated references in file edits. This addresses incidents from the usage report where Claude fabricated URLs, used wrong logo assets, and misidentified GA4 accounts.

**PREREQUISITE: Read task claude-code-8g8 first. Complete task claude-code-d6g first.**

**Implementation:**
- File: hooks/verify-before-edit.sh
- ALSO create: claude-dev-toolkit/hooks/verify-before-edit.sh (identical copy)

**Hook must follow this exact metadata format (required by hook-metadata-service.js):**
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# Claude Code Hook: Verify Before Edit
#
# Description: Warns about potentially fabricated references in file edits
# Purpose: Prevent use of fabricated URLs, account IDs, or asset paths by validating references
# Trigger: PreToolUse
# Blocking: No
# Tools: Edit, Write, MultiEdit
# Author: Claude Dev Toolkit
# Version: 1.0.0
# Category: security
#
# This hook checks for placeholder or fabricated identifiers to prevent
# accidental use of invalid references in code and configuration files.
```

**CRITICAL: Input mechanism — uses environment variables, NOT stdin**
Claude Code sets these env vars before invoking hooks:
- \$CLAUDE_TOOL — tool name (Edit, Write, MultiEdit)
- \$CLAUDE_FILE — the file being edited (check if it exists)
- \$CLAUDE_CONTENT — the content being written (use this to scan for suspicious patterns)
To scan content: use \$CLAUDE_CONTENT if set, otherwise read from \$CLAUDE_FILE if it exists.

**CRITICAL: Security keyword requirement**
hook-validator.js requires at least one of: credential, security, validate, check, prevent. The hook naturally contains 'validate' and 'check' — ensure these words appear in comments or code.

**Behavior: NON-BLOCKING (always exits 0)**
- Scans \$CLAUDE_CONTENT for suspicious patterns:
  - URLs containing 'example', 'placeholder', 'your-', 'xxx', 'TODO'
  - Account IDs that are all zeros or sequential (000000, 123456)
  - Common placeholder patterns: 'your-api-key-here', 'REPLACE_ME', '<INSERT>'
- Outputs warnings to stderr for any suspicious patterns found
- Logs all findings to ~/.claude/logs/verify-before-edit.log
- Always exits 0 — informational only, never blocks
- Skip scanning for files matching: *test*, *spec*, *example*, *fixture*, *mock*

**Files to create/modify:**
1. Create hooks/verify-before-edit.sh
2. Copy to claude-dev-toolkit/hooks/verify-before-edit.sh
3. chmod +x both files
4. Update hooks/README.md — add entry for verify-before-edit
5. Update claude-dev-toolkit/tests/test_npm_package_completeness.js:
   - Add 'hooks/verify-before-edit.sh' to both expectedHooks arrays (lines ~115 and ~267)
   (NOTE: these arrays were already expanded by d6g — just append)
6. Add hook reference to templates/comprehensive-settings.json under PreToolUse with matcher 'Edit|Write|MultiEdit'
7. Add hook reference to templates/security-focused-settings.json under PreToolUse with matcher 'Edit|Write|MultiEdit'
8. Copy updated settings templates to claude-dev-toolkit/templates/

**Verification:**
```bash
test -x hooks/verify-before-edit.sh && echo 'OK: repo' || echo 'MISSING: repo'
test -x claude-dev-toolkit/hooks/verify-before-edit.sh && echo 'OK: npm' || echo 'MISSING: npm'
cd claude-dev-toolkit && npm test
```
close_reason: Created verify-before-edit.sh (non-blocking, env var input, placeholder detection). Dual-written, added to settings templates, test hooks updated.

title: Fix ShellCheck warnings and shell hygiene across scripts
description:
LOW-11: SC2155 warnings (declare and assign separately) in hooks/file-logger.sh:80-81, hooks/lib/subagent-validator.sh:494,560,561, scripts/deploy-subagents.sh:293. Split local var=$(cmd) into local var; var=$(cmd).
LOW-12: SC2207 warnings in hooks/lib/subagent-discovery.sh:64,108. Use mapfile instead of $(sort ...) for array population.
LOW-13: Publishing scripts (publish-private.sh, publish-public.sh, setup-local-registry.sh, test-package-install.sh) use set -e only, not set -euo pipefail. Upgrade for consistency with hooks.

title: Add inline comments for iptables generation logic

title: Update stale model ID in settings templates

The comprehensive-settings.json template has a stale model reference: claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022. Update to current model IDs.

**PREREQUISITE: Read task claude-code-8g8 first. Complete task claude-code-d6g first (it may modify the same file).**

   "// Model options: claude-sonnet-4-6 (default), claude-opus-4-6 (most capable), claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 (fastest)": ""

**Additional checks:**
- Search templates/basic-settings.json and templates/security-focused-settings.json for any model references — update if found
- Search the repo root CLAUDE.md for stale model references (grep for 'claude-3-5' or 'claude-3-opus' or 'sonnet-20241022')

2. Copy updated file to claude-dev-toolkit/templates/comprehensive-settings.json
3. Check and update any other templates if stale model refs found

**Verification:**

```bash
grep -r 'claude-3-5-sonnet' templates/ && echo 'STALE FOUND' || echo 'OK: no stale refs'
grep -r 'claude-3-5-sonnet' claude-dev-toolkit/templates/ && echo 'STALE FOUND' || echo 'OK: no stale refs'
# Template is valid JSON
python3 -c 'import json; json.load(open("templates/comprehensive-settings.json"))' && echo 'OK: valid JSON'
# npm tests pass
cd claude-dev-toolkit && npm test
```
close_reason: Updated model ID to claude-sonnet-4-6, added model options comment. No other stale refs found.

title: Fix code quality: ShellCheck errors, decompose large files, remove unused deps
description:
HIGH-11: ShellCheck SC1073/SC1078/SC1083 parse errors in prevent-credential-exposure.sh:71-96 on declare -A CREDENTIAL_PATTERNS. Prevents analysis of rest of file. Refactor to separate declare/assign or external data file.
HIGH-12: 8+ shell scripts over 400 lines (deploy-subagents.sh 669, execution-engine.sh 626, subagent-validator.sh 596) and 8+ JS files over 500 lines (oidc-command.js 739, error-recovery-system.js 562). Decompose via Extract Class/Module.
MED-09: Jest declared as devDependency in both package.json files but never used. All tests use custom runners. Either migrate or remove.
close_reason: Duplicate of claude-code-06j and claude-code-m27 (HIGH-11, HIGH-12, HIGH-13)

title: Add functional tests for hooks, hooks/lib, and slash commands
description:
HIGH-05: 22 hooks have zero dedicated functional tests (only test_subagent_hook_integration.sh tests subagent-trigger.sh). 8 hooks/lib modules have zero unit tests (CI only does bash -n syntax checks). 15 active slash commands have zero behavioral tests — existing tests only check file existence and markdown patterns.
HIGH-06: ~80+ REQ-xxx requirements across specs. Only 8 have corresponding tests. Entire spec files have zero coverage: claude-commands-config-requirements.md, claude-commands-hooks-requirements.md, claude-commands-subagents-requirements.md, npm-consolidation/ (30+ requirements), npm-ssm-requirements.md, claude-dev-toolkit-oidc-requirements.md.
Fix: Prioritize tests for security-critical hooks (prevent-credential-exposure.sh, pre-write-security.sh). Add unit tests for hooks/lib modules. Add behavioral tests for core slash commands. Create a traceability matrix for spec-to-test coverage.

title: Fix credential scanner bypass: replace CLAUDE_SECURITY_OVERRIDE env var
description:
CRIT-02: Setting CLAUDE_SECURITY_OVERRIDE=true in hooks/prevent-credential-exposure.sh:244-248 bypasses all credential scanning. The override persists via config-constants.sh:217 and bypass instructions are printed to stdout at line 239.
Fix: Replace persistent env var with per-invocation token or time-limited override. Remove bypass instruction from stdout. Add tests for the bypass code path (currently zero test coverage for this critical security path).
close_reason: Removed CLAUDE_SECURITY_OVERRIDE bypass from prevent-credential-exposure.sh, config-constants.sh, and context-manager.sh (both root and claude-dev-toolkit copies)
